Name: Retatrutide
Short description: A triple-agonist peptide targeting GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors for advanced metabolic research. For research use only.
Description: Retatrutide is a novel investigational triple-agonist peptide that simultaneously targets GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors. This tri-receptor approach represents the latest generation of metabolic research compounds, building on the dual-agonist framework established by earlier peptides.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized retatrutide powder at the selected strength. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: Tirzepatide
Short description: A dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist peptide for metabolic and endocrine research. For research use only.
Description: Tirzepatide is a dual-acting peptide that targets both GLP-1 and GIP receptors. Originally developed for metabolic research, this compound has become one of the most studied peptides in the glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide pathway.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized tirzepatide powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: Semaglutide
Short description: A GLP-1 receptor agonist peptide widely studied in metabolic and appetite regulation research. For research use only.
Description: Semaglutide is a potent, long-acting GLP-1 receptor agonist peptide. It features an acylated side chain that extends its half-life, making it one of the most extensively studied GLP-1 analogs in metabolic research.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized semaglutide powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: Tesamorelin
Short description: A synthetic GHRH analog peptide studied for its role in growth hormone release and body composition research. For research use only.
Description: Tesamorelin is a synthetic analog of growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH). It consists of the full 44-amino-acid sequence of human GHRH with a trans-3-hexenoic acid modification, which enhances its stability and bioactivity in research settings.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized tesamorelin powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: MOTS-c
Short description: A mitochondrial-derived peptide studied for its role in cellular energy metabolism and exercise mimetic research. For research use only.
Description: MOTS-c (Mitochondrial Open Reading Frame of the Twelve S rRNA type-c) is a 16-amino-acid peptide encoded by mitochondrial DNA. It is one of a small number of known mitochondrial-derived peptides (MDPs) and has attracted significant research interest for its role in regulating metabolic homeostasis and AMPK pathway activation.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized MOTS-c powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: BPC-157
Short description: A pentadecapeptide derived from gastric juice proteins, studied extensively in tissue repair and cytoprotection research. For research use only.
Description: BPC-157 (Body Protection Compound-157) is a 15-amino-acid peptide derived from a portion of the human gastric juice protein. It is one of the most widely studied peptides in the tissue repair and cytoprotection research space, with a broad range of published studies examining its role in wound healing, tendon repair, and gastrointestinal protection.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized BPC-157 powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: GHK-Cu
Short description: A copper-binding tripeptide studied for its roles in skin remodeling, wound healing, and anti-aging research. For research use only.
Description: GHK-Cu (glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine copper complex) is a naturally occurring copper-binding tripeptide found in human plasma. Research interest in GHK-Cu centers on its roles in collagen synthesis, fibroblast activity, and gene expression modulation related to tissue remodeling.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized GHK-Cu powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: NAD+
Short description: Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide — a coenzyme central to cellular energy metabolism and sirtuin activation research. For research use only.
Description: NAD+ (nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide) is a coenzyme present in all living cells that plays a central role in energy metabolism, DNA repair, and sirtuin activation. Research into NAD+ supplementation has expanded rapidly, with studies examining its potential in age-related decline, mitochondrial function, and neuroprotection.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized NAD+ powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: KLOW Blend
Short description: A four-peptide GH support blend in a single vial. Pre-mixed and pre-dosed — no math, no mixing, no fuss. For research use only.
Description: KLOW Blend is a four-peptide growth hormone support blend combining CJC-1295, Ipamorelin, GHRP-2, and GHRP-6 in a single pre-dosed vial. Formulated by Natty Vision developed exclusively by Natty Vision. This blend combines complementary peptide compounds in a single lyophilized vial for streamlined research workflows.\n\nEach vial contains 80mg total peptide content. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications. Formulation details available upon request for qualified researchers.

Name: TB-500
Short description: A synthetic fragment of thymosin beta-4 studied for its roles in tissue repair, inflammation, and cell migration research. For research use only.
Description: TB-500 is a synthetic peptide fragment of thymosin beta-4, a 43-amino-acid protein involved in cell migration, blood vessel formation, and tissue repair. TB-500 research focuses on its roles in upregulating actin and promoting cell motility, which are key processes in wound healing and recovery.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized TB-500 powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: Semax
Short description: A synthetic heptapeptide analog of ACTH(4-10) studied in cognitive enhancement and neuroprotection research. For research use only.
Description: Semax is a synthetic heptapeptide derived from ACTH(4-10) with a Pro-Gly-Pro C-terminal modification that enhances its stability. Originally developed in Russia, it has been the subject of extensive nootropic and neuroprotection research, with studies examining BDNF modulation, cognitive enhancement, and neuroplasticity.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized Semax powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

This blend combines complementary peptide compounds targeting skin and tissue research pathways in a single lyophilized vial.\n\nEach vial contains 70mg total peptide content. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications. Formulation details available upon request for qualified researchers.

Name: BPC/TB Blend
Short description: A combined BPC-157 and TB-500 blend for synergistic tissue repair and recovery research. For research use only.
Description: This BPC/TB Blend combines two of the most studied tissue repair peptides — BPC-157 (5mg) and TB-500 (5mg) — in a single lyophilized vial for 10mg total peptide content. Researchers studying these compounds together benefit from pre-blended convenience and consistent ratios.\n\nEach vial contains 5mg BPC-157 and 5mg TB-500. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: Melanotan 2
Short description: A synthetic melanocortin peptide analog studied in pigmentation and melanocortin receptor research. For research use only.
Description: Melanotan II (MT-2) is a synthetic cyclic lactam analog of alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α-MSH). It is a non-selective agonist of melanocortin receptors MC1R through MC5R and has been extensively studied in pigmentation, appetite, and related melanocortin pathway research.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized Melanotan II powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: SS-31
Short description: A mitochondrial-targeted tetrapeptide studied for its role in oxidative stress reduction and mitochondrial membrane stabilization. For research use only.
Description: SS-31 (also known as elamipretide or Bendavia) is a cell-permeable tetrapeptide that selectively targets the inner mitochondrial membrane. It binds to cardiolipin, a phospholipid critical to electron transport chain function, and has been studied for its role in reducing reactive oxygen species production and protecting mitochondrial structure.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized SS-31 powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: Selank
Short description: A synthetic heptapeptide analog of tuftsin studied in anxiolytic, nootropic, and immunomodulatory research. For research use only.
Description: Selank is a synthetic heptapeptide derived from the immunomodulatory peptide tuftsin with a Pro-Gly-Pro C-terminal extension for enhanced stability. Developed at the Institute of Molecular Genetics (Russian Academy of Sciences), it has been studied for anxiolytic, nootropic, and immunomodulatory properties.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized Selank powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: CJC-1295 No DAC + Ipamorelin
Short description: A combined growth hormone secretagogue blend of CJC-1295 (no DAC) and Ipamorelin for synergistic GH research. For research use only.
Description: This blend combines CJC-1295 without DAC (Drug Affinity Complex) and Ipamorelin, two of the most studied growth hormone secretagogues, in a single lyophilized vial. CJC-1295 no DAC is a modified GHRH analog, while Ipamorelin is a selective ghrelin mimetic. Together they are studied for their synergistic effects on pulsatile GH release patterns.\n\nEach vial contains CJC-1295 no DAC 5mg and Ipamorelin 5mg (10mg total). Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: KPV
Short description: A tripeptide fragment of α-MSH studied for its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties in research settings. For research use only.
Description: KPV (Lys-Pro-Val) is a naturally occurring tripeptide corresponding to the C-terminal fragment of alpha-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α-MSH). Despite its small size, KPV retains the anti-inflammatory signaling properties of the parent hormone and has been studied for its effects on NF-κB inhibition and inflammatory cytokine modulation.\n\nEach vial contains lyophilized KPV powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.
